page: UpCloud GPU-96xCPU-960GB-4xB200 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 CPU architecture with 96 shared vCPUs and 960.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 10.0 GB per vCPU.

The primary hardware differentiator of this instance is its integrated graphics processing unit array. It is equipped with four NVIDIA B200 GPUs from the Blackwell family, providing a combined total of 768 GB of VRAM. This configuration does not include local storage, meaning storage resources must be attached externally.

Given the high-density GPU and memory allocation, this server is designed for resource-intensive workloads. The shared CPU allocation represents a specific architectural tradeoff, positioning the instance for tasks that rely heavily on GPU acceleration rather than sustained, dedicated single-core CPU performance. It is suited for large-scale machine learning, deep learning model training, large language model inference, and high-performance data science computations.
description: UpCloud GPU-96xCPU-960GB-4xB200 is a GPU-accelerated virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. Utilizing the x86_64 CPU architecture, the instance provides 96 shared vCPUs and 960.0 GB of system memory, establishing a 10.0 GB per core ratio. The core capability of this server lies in its GPU configuration, which features four NVIDIA B200 Blackwell GPUs delivering a total of 768 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, requiring external storage attachments. Given the shared CPU allocation, the hardware profile is optimized to offload heavy computational tasks to the GPU array rather than relying on dedicated host CPU cores. This makes the server highly suitable for large-scale artificial intelligence workloads, deep learning training, complex data science jobs, and large language model inference.
